snippets: The Amberg-Sulzbach region offers a wide range of hiking opportunities.
The region promotes experiencing nature directly.
Hiking routes are available in and around Amberg.
The Skulpturenweg is a circular walking route in Amberg.
The Skulpturenweg starts at the Stadtbrille.
The Skulpturenweg ends at the Stadtbrille.
The Skulpturenweg has a length of 3.5 kilometers.
The Skulpturenweg is located on the former Landesgartenschau grounds.
The route features artworks from the State Garden Show.
Additional artworks were added later to the route.
An information board at the Stadtbrille explains the route.
A map of the Skulpturenweg is available at the Tourist Information.
The Bodenerlebnispfad is located on the Mariahilfberg.
The Bodenerlebnispfad has a length of 1.5 kilometers.
The trail includes ten stations.
The trail allows visitors to physically interact with soil.
The trail explains how soil is formed.
The trail explains the importance of soil.
The trail explores life within the soil.
The Wasserwanderweg is a walking route in Amberg.
The Wasserwanderweg focuses on the city’s water supply system.
The Erzweg is a long-distance hiking trail.
The Erzweg consists of 11 stages.
The Erzweg includes 14 circular routes.
The Erzweg passes through meadows and fields.
The Erzweg passes through forest landscapes.
The Erzweg includes rocky terrain.
The Erzweg connects small villages.
The Erzweg passes through agricultural areas.
The Erzweg includes natural landscapes.
The Erzweg includes paths along streams.
The Erzweg includes narrow hiking trails.
The Erzweg passes by chapels.
The Erzweg passes by traditional inns.
Dolomite rock formations are present along the Erzweg.
Limestone formations are present along the Erzweg.
The Jurasteig is a long-distance hiking trail.
The Jurasteig has a total length of 237 kilometers.
The Jurasteig is divided into 13 daily stages.
The Jurasteig forms a circular route.
The Jurasteig passes through the Bavarian Jura.
The Jurasteig crosses the Danube river region.
The Jurasteig crosses the Altmühl valley.
The Jurasteig crosses the Vils valley.
The Jurasteig crosses the Naab river region.
The Jurasteig crosses the Lauterach valley.
The Jurasteig crosses the Weißer Laber river.
The Jurasteig crosses the Schwarzer Laber river.
The Jurasteig includes 17 additional loop trails.
The Jurasteig has received the Qualitätsweg Wanderbares Deutschland certification.
The Jurasteig passes through areas with peregrine falcons.
The Jurasteig passes through areas with kingfishers.
The Jurasteig includes orchid-rich landscapes.
The Jurasteig includes plant species such as Carthusian Pink.
The Jurasteig includes plant species such as quaking grass.
The Wacholderwanderweg is a hiking trail.
The Wacholderwanderweg has a length of approximately 31 kilometers.
The trail runs from Schmidmühlen to Kastl.
The trail passes through juniper landscapes.
The landscape includes dry grasslands.
The landscape is maintained by grazing sheep.
The trail passes along the Lauterach river.
The trail includes orchid-rich areas.
The trail has characteristics similar to Mediterranean landscapes.
Short walking routes are available around Amberg.
The Wacholderwanderweg durchs Hochholz is a circular route.
The route has a length of approximately 5 kilometers.
The route starts at Berghof Kastl.
The route offers views of the Lauterach valley.
The Felsenlabyrinth von Sackdilling is a circular hiking route.
The route has a length of 2.6 kilometers.
The Felsensteig Schwend is a circular hiking route.
The route has a length of 3.9 kilometers.
The Rundweg Rote Raute is a circular hiking route.
The route passes through the Görgltal.
The route has a length of 4.5 kilometers.
The Auerochsentour is a circular hiking route.
The Auerochsentour has a length of 2.7 kilometers.
The route can be completed in approximately 45 minutes.
The Neukirchner Höhlenweg is a circular hiking route.
The route has a length of 4.4 kilometers.
The route takes approximately 1 hour and 20 minutes.
The route from Rieden to Burgruine Roßstein is a circular hike.
The route has a length of 14.3 kilometers.
The route takes approximately 3 hours and 50 minutes.
The Felsentour zur Osterhöhle is a circular route.
The route has a length of 6.7 kilometers.
The route takes approximately 2 hours.
Longer day hikes are available in the region.
The Hirschauer Turmweg is a circular route.
The route has a length of 16.1 kilometers.
The route takes approximately 4 hours and 35 minutes.
The Hirschwald-Schlaufe is a circular route.
The route has a length of 19.2 kilometers.
The route takes approximately 5 hours and 20 minutes.
